Bed 1.name: Angel Wings
Bed 1.care: Avoid getting water on the velvety leaves. Protect from hard frosts.
Bed 1.seasonal: Evergreen in mild areas. Offers stunning silver foliage mostly spring to autumn.

Bed 1.name: Astilbe
Bed 1.water: Moist to wet soil; do not let dry out.
Bed 1.care: Leave dried flower heads for winter interest or cut back in autumn.
Bed 1.seasonal: Herbaceous perennial. Dies back in winter. Feathery flower plumes appear in summer.

Bed 1.name: Box Hedging
Bed 1.water: Tolerates most well-drained soils. Water regularly when newly planted; established plants are fairly drought-tolerant.
Bed 1.care: Clip twice a year (late spring and late summer) to maintain a neat shape. Watch for box blight and box tree caterpillar.
Bed 1.seasonal: Evergreen. Retains dense, glossy green foliage year-round. Produces small insignificant flowers in spring.

Bed 1.name: English Daisy
Bed 1.care: Deadhead spent flowers to encourage continued blooming. Can self-seed freely.
Bed 1.seasonal: Short-lived perennial often grown as a biennial. Produces pink, red, or white pom-pom flowers from early spring through summer. May die back in hot summers.

Bed 1.name: Euonymus
Bed 1.care: Prune in spring to maintain shape. Can be used as ground cover or trained upwards.
Bed 1.seasonal: Evergreen. Keeps its bright golden-variegated foliage all year round. Colours may intensify in full sun.

Bed 1.name: Hosta (Plantain Lily)
Bed 1.care: Watch out for slugs and snails!
Bed 1.seasonal: Herbaceous perennial. Dies back underground in winter. Lush leaves emerge in spring.

Bed 1.name: Japanese Aralia
Bed 1.seasonal: Evergreen. Keeps its speckled tropical leaves year-round.

Bed 1.name: Japanese Maple
Bed 1.water: Keep soil consistently moist but not waterlogged.
Bed 1.seasonal: Requires minimal pruning. Remove dead or crossing branches during the dormant winter season.

Bed 1.name: Rhododendron
Bed 1.care: Deadhead carefully after flowering. Needs ericaceous compost/fertiliser.
Bed 1.seasonal: Evergreen. Keeps its variegated leaves all year. Blooms with purple flowers in late spring.

Bed 2.name: Angel Wings
Bed 2.care: Avoid getting water on the velvety leaves. Protect from hard frosts.
Bed 2.seasonal: Evergreen in mild areas. Offers stunning silver foliage mostly spring to autumn.

Bed 2.name: Forget-me-not
Bed 2.seasonal: Pull up plants after flowering to prevent excessive self-seeding, or leave them if naturalising is desired.

Bed 2.name: Hosta (Plantain Lily)
Bed 2.care: Watch out for slugs and snails!
Bed 2.seasonal: Herbaceous perennial. Dies back underground in winter. Lush leaves emerge in spring.

Bed 2.name: Maiden Pink
Bed 2.seasonal: Evergreen mat of foliage. Blooms with bright pink/red flowers in summer.

Bed 2.name: Nemesia
Bed 2.water: Regular; keep the soil moist but ensure it does not become soggy.
Bed 2.seasonal: Trim the plant back slightly after the initial flush of flowers to encourage a second display.

Bed 2.name: Peony
Bed 2.care: Provide plant supports for heavy flower heads. Cut back dead foliage in late autumn.
Bed 2.seasonal: Herbaceous perennial. Dies back completely in winter. Huge blooms in late spring/early summer.

Bed 2.name: Silverbush
Bed 2.care: Protect from severe winter wet and cold.
Bed 2.seasonal: Evergreen. Retains silver foliage year-round. Produces white/pink flowers in spring and summer.

Bed 2.name: Variegated Dogwood
Bed 2.water: Tolerates most soils even damp ones.
Bed 2.care: Prune hard (coppice) in late winter/early spring to encourage bright new stems.
Bed 2.seasonal: Deciduous. Drops leaves in autumn to reveal striking bright red winter stems.

Bed 2.name: Weeping Cherry
Bed 2.water: Needs moderately fertile, well-drained soil. Keep the ground consistently moist but avoid waterlogged conditions.
Bed 2.care: Prune during the dormant winter period only to remove dead, diseased, or crossing branches. Avoid disrupting the natural weeping form. Apply a balanced fertiliser in early spring.
Bed 2.seasonal: Deciduous. Exhibits prolific spring blossoms before or alongside leaf emergence. The canopy provides summer shade before leaves turn yellow or bronze and drop in autumn.

Bed 2.name: Weigela
Bed 2.seasonal: Deciduous. Drops leaves in autumn. Blooms with trumpet flowers in late spring/early summer.

Bed 3.name: Apple Tree
Bed 3.water: Regular watering, particularly during fruit development.
Bed 3.seasonal: Prune in late winter while dormant to maintain structure and encourage fruiting spurs.

Bed 3.name: Avens
Bed 3.water: Moist but well-drained soil; water regularly.
Bed 3.care: Deadhead spent flowers to encourage more blooms.
Bed 3.seasonal: Herbaceous or semi-evergreen. Produces bright flowers from late spring through summer.

Bed 3.name: Wintercreeper
Bed 3.care: Prune in spring to maintain shape.
Bed 3.seasonal: Evergreen shrub. Keeps its bright variegated foliage all year round.

Bed 4.name: Lavender
Bed 4.water: Well-drained, poor to moderately fertile soil. Drought-tolerant once established.
Bed 4.care: Prune after flowering in late summer, cutting back to just above the woody growth. Do not cut into old wood.
Bed 4.seasonal: Evergreen. Grey-green aromatic foliage year-round. Produces fragrant purple flower spikes from mid to late summer. Attractive to bees and butterflies.

Bed 4.name: New Zealand Flax
Bed 4.water: Moderate; keep evenly moist during the growing season.
Bed 4.seasonal: Cut back old, damaged, or untidy leaves right to the base in spring.

Bed 4.name: Rose
Bed 4.water: Regular, deep watering at the base to avoid wetting the foliage.
Bed 4.seasonal: Prune hard in late winter or early spring. Deadhead spent blooms regularly to encourage further flowering.

Bed 4.name: Wisteria
Bed 4.water: Regular watering until a deep root system is established.
Bed 4.seasonal: Requires bi-annual pruning (summer and winter) to control vigorous growth and promote flower buds.

Bed 4.name: Yucca
Bed 4.water: Very well-drained soil; highly drought-tolerant once established.
Bed 4.care: Low maintenance. Remove dead or damaged leaves at the base. Wear gloves as leaf tips can be sharp.
Bed 4.seasonal: Evergreen. Spiky architectural rosettes provide year-round structure. Produces tall spikes of creamy-white bell flowers in summer.

Stone Bed.name: Aubrieta (Rock Cress)
Stone Bed.care: Shear back after blooming to keep it compact.
Stone Bed.seasonal: Evergreen foliage. Bursts with purple flowers in spring.

Stone Bed.name: Cabbage Tree
Stone Bed.seasonal: Remove dead or yellowing leaves from the base of the tufts to maintain a tidy appearance.

Stone Bed.name: Honeysuckle
Stone Bed.water: Adaptable to various soil types, provided they are moist and well-drained. Water regularly during the first growing season.
Stone Bed.care: Prune after flowering to control growth and remove congested stems. Train new shoots along supports as required.
Stone Bed.seasonal: Deciduous or semi-evergreen depending on the exact species. Characterised by rapid summer growth and fragrant tubular flowers, often followed by small berries in autumn.

Stone Bed.name: Houseleeks (Hens and Chicks)
Stone Bed.care: Very low maintenance. Remove dead rosettes after they flower and die.
Stone Bed.seasonal: Evergreen rosettes. Forms offsets (chicks) during growing season.

Stone Bed.name: New Zealand Flax (dark)
Stone Bed.water: Moderate; keep evenly moist during the growing season. Versatile; prefers well-drained conditions.
Stone Bed.care: Cut back old, damaged, or untidy leaves right to the base in spring. Protect crown from severe frost with mulch.
Stone Bed.seasonal: Evergreen. Dark burgundy-black sword-shaped leaves provide dramatic year-round contrast against the grey slate chippings.

Stone Bed.name: Rosemary
Stone Bed.water: Low; highly drought-tolerant once established. Allow soil to dry between waterings.
Stone Bed.care: Poor to moderately fertile, very well-drained soil.
Stone Bed.seasonal: Prune lightly after flowering to maintain shape and prevent the centre from becoming overly woody.

Stone Bed.name: Stonecrop
Stone Bed.care: Low maintenance. Trim back if it gets too leggy.
Stone Bed.seasonal: Mostly evergreen. Flowers in summer. Leaf colours may deepen in cooler weather.

Patio.name: Clematis
Patio.water: Requires moist, well-drained earth. Deep watering is necessary during extended dry spells.
Patio.care: Pruning depends on the specific group; spring-flowering types generally need minimal pruning immediately after flowering to control size. Mulch around the base to keep roots cool.
Patio.seasonal: Deciduous. Produces vigorous growth and abundant flowers in spring. Foliage drops in autumn, leaving bare woody stems over winter.

Tree.name: Pear Tree
Tree.water: Prefers well-drained, loamy soil. Requires regular watering during the establishment phase and prolonged dry periods, particularly when fruiting.
Tree.care: Requires annual winter pruning to maintain shape, remove dead wood, and encourage productive fruiting spurs. Monitor for common pests like aphids and diseases such as pear rust.
Tree.seasonal: Deciduous. Blossoms in early spring, followed by leaf emergence and fruit development through summer. Harvest typically occurs in late summer or autumn, preceding leaf fall.
